title: Drug-Drug Interactions
summary: التفاعلات الدوائية هي تغيرات في تأثير دواء ما عند إعطائه مع دواء آخر. تنقسم إلى تفاعلات صيدلانية (خارج الجسم)، وحرائكية (تؤثر على الامتصاص والتوزيع والاستقلاب والإفراز)، وديناميكية (تؤثر على مستوى المستقبلات والأجهزة). أهم العوامل المؤهبة: كبر السن، وتعدد الأدوية، وضعف وظائف الكلى والكبد. معرفة الأدوية المحرضة والمثبطة للإنزيمات الكبدية أمر جوهري لتجنب التسمم أو فقدان الفعالية.

key_drugs.name: Rifampin
key_drugs.note: محرِّض قوي لـ CYP2C و CYP3A يقلل فعالية حبوب منع الحمل والوارفارين

key_drugs.name: Warfarin
key_drugs.note: دواء ذو نافذة علاجية ضيقة، شديد الحساسية للتفاعلات الدوائية

key_drugs.name: Ketoconazole
key_drugs.note: مثبط CYP450 يزيد مستوى الوارفارين وأدوية أخرى

key_drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
key_drugs.note: يثبط CYP450 ويرتبط بالتيتراسيكلين ويقلل امتصاصه مع الأنتاسيدات

key_drugs.name: Metoclopramide
key_drugs.note: يزيد إفراغ المعدة فيزيد امتصاص البروبرانولول والباراسيتامول

key_drugs.name: Probenecid
key_drugs.note: يثبط الإفراز النبيبي للبنسلين فيطيل مفعوله

key_drugs.name: Cholestyramine
key_drugs.note: يمتز الوارفارين والديجوكسين والثيروكسين في الأمعاء

key_drugs.name: Phenytoin
key_drugs.note: محرِّض إنزيمي يقلل فعالية حبوب منع الحمل

key_drugs.name: Aspirin
key_drugs.note: يزيح الأدوية الأخرى من بروتينات البلازما ويثبط إفراز الميثوتريكسات الكلوي

mnemonics.title: SPEC-RD - محرضات الإنزيمات الكبدية
mnemonics.body:
S = Smoking (التدخين)
P = Phenytoin (فينيتوين)
E = Ethanol (الكحول)
C = Carbamazepine (كاربامازيبين)
R = Rifampin (ريفامبيسين)
D = Dexamethasone (ديكساميثازون)
وأيضاً Barbiturates. كل هذه تُحرِّض CYP450 وتُقلل مستوى الوارفارين وحبوب منع الحمل.

mnemonics.title: GECKO - مثبطات الإنزيمات الكبدية
mnemonics.body:
G = Grapefruit juice (عصير الجريب فروت)
E = Erythromycin (إريثروميسين)
C = Cimetidine (سيميتيدين)
K = Ketoconazole (كيتوكونازول)
O = Omeprazole + Quinidine + Ritonavir + Ciprofloxacin + Sulfonamides
هذه تُثبط CYP450 وتُراكم الأدوية المتعاملة معها مما يؤدي إلى التسمم.

mnemonics.title: الجبن والـ MAOI - قصة التيرامين
mnemonics.body: تخيّل شخصاً يتناول دواء الاكتئاب (MAOI) ويأكل الجبن (cheese). الجبن يحتوي على تيرامين (tyramine) الذي يُكسَّر عادةً بإنزيم MAO في الأمعاء. لكن مع تثبيط MAO، يتراكم التيرامين ويُطلق كميات ضخمة من الكاتيكولامينات مما يُسبب أزمة ارتفاع ضغط الدم الحادة. الجبن + MAOI = خطر!

mnemonics.title: تصوير إزاحة البروتين - الأسبرين والوارفارين
mnemonics.body: تخيّل بروتين الألبومين كـ'حافلة' مليئة بالركاب (الوارفارين المرتبط). يأتي الأسبرين كـ'زاحف قوي' يُزيح ركاب الوارفارين من مقاعدهم، فيُصبحون أحراراً في الدم (free warfarin). الوارفارين الحر أكثر خطورة: خطر النزيف يرتفع.

mnemonics.title: بروبينسيد والبنسلين - حارس الكلى
mnemonics.body: تخيّل البروبينسيد (Probenecid) كـ'حارس أمين' يقف أمام باب الإفراز النبيبي ويمنع البنسلين من الخروج في البول. النتيجة: البنسلين يبقى في الجسم أطول ومستواه يرتفع. هذا مفيد أحياناً لإطالة مفعول المضاد الحيوي.

flashcards.front: What are the predisposing factors for drug-drug interactions?
flashcards.back: Advanced age, polypharmacy, severe acute illness, impaired renal and hepatic function, more than one prescribing physician.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between the Precipitant drug and the Object drug in interactions?
flashcards.back: The precipitant is the drug that causes the interaction (e.g., Rifampin); the object is the drug whose concentration or effect is altered (e.g., Warfarin).

flashcards.front: How does Metoclopramide affect drug absorption?
flashcards.back: It increases gastric motility and accelerates gastric emptying, thereby increasing absorption of Propranolol and Paracetamol.

flashcards.front: Why do antacids (Mg++, Al+++) reduce the efficacy of Tetracycline and Ciprofloxacin?
flashcards.back: Because they form chelation complexes with these drugs, preventing their absorption from the gastrointestinal tract.

flashcards.front: What is the dangerous interaction between Aspirin and Warfarin?
flashcards.back: Aspirin displaces Warfarin from its plasma protein binding sites, raising the free Warfarin fraction and increasing the risk of bleeding.

flashcards.front: Name five CYP450 enzyme inducers.
flashcards.back: Rifampin, Phenytoin, Carbamazepine, Barbiturates, Dexamethasone, Ethanol, Smoking.

flashcards.front: Name five CYP450 enzyme inhibitors.
flashcards.back: Ketoconazole, Erythromycin, Ciprofloxacin, Cimetidine, Grapefruit juice, Ritonavir, Omeprazole.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of co-administering Rifampin with oral contraceptives?
flashcards.back: Rifampin induces CYP2C and CYP3A, increasing the metabolism of the contraceptive hormones, causing contraceptive failure.

flashcards.front: What is 'ion trapping' in renal excretion?
flashcards.back: Acidifying the urine increases excretion of weak bases (amphetamine); alkalinizing the urine increases excretion of weak acids (aspirin, phenobarbital).

flashcards.front: What is the interaction between MAOI and SSRI?
flashcards.back: A very dangerous interaction: leads to serotonin syndrome (fever, muscle rigidity, coma).

flashcards.front: Why is Probenecid given with Penicillin?
flashcards.back: Probenecid inhibits the tubular secretion of Penicillin in the kidneys, raising its blood level and prolonging its effect.

flashcards.front: What is the interaction between 6-mercaptopurine and Allopurinol?
flashcards.back: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase, which breaks down 6-MP, causing its accumulation and worsening bone marrow suppression.

flashcards.front: Why should Ampicillin, Chlorpromazine, and Barbiturates not be added to Dextran solutions?
flashcards.back: Because they react chemically with Dextran, forming complexes that cause loss of drug activity.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of Verapamil combined with beta-blockers?
flashcards.back: A synergistic effect causing severe bradycardia, which may progress to asystole.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of the neuromuscular blocker–aminoglycoside interaction?
flashcards.back: Aminoglycosides potentiate neuromuscular blockade, prolonging muscle paralysis after anaesthesia.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of Cholestyramine on drug absorption?
flashcards.back: It binds Warfarin, Digoxin, and Thyroxine in the intestine, preventing their absorption and reducing their efficacy.

flashcards.front: What is the interaction between NSAIDs and ACE inhibitors?
flashcards.back: NSAIDs cause sodium retention and attenuate the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors.

flashcards.front: What is the danger of combining ACE inhibitors with K+-sparing diuretics?
flashcards.back: Both raise potassium levels; combining them causes dangerous hyperkalemia.

flashcards.front: What are the predisposing factors for drug-drug interactions?
flashcards.back: Advanced age, polypharmacy, acute-on-chronic illness, impaired renal/hepatic function, multiple prescribing physicians.

flashcards.front: What are the characteristics of drugs that are commonly the 'object' in an interaction?
flashcards.back: Steep dose-response curve and narrow therapeutic index (narrow TI).

flashcards.front: What are the main types of drug-drug interactions?
flashcards.back: Pharmaceutical (outside the body), Pharmacokinetic (ADME), Pharmacodynamic (same or opposing effect).

flashcards.front: How does plasma protein displacement cause an interaction?
flashcards.back: A highly protein-bound drug displaces another from albumin binding sites → ↑ free fraction → toxicity.

flashcards.front: List hepatic enzyme inducers.
flashcards.back: Rifampicin, Phenytoin, Carbamazepine, Barbiturates, chronic Ethanol.

flashcards.front: List hepatic enzyme inhibitors.
flashcards.back: Cimetidine, Ketoconazole, Erythromycin, Grapefruit juice.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of an enzyme inducer on oral contraceptive efficacy?
flashcards.back: Reduces their concentration → contraceptive failure (unintended pregnancy).

flashcards.front: Give an example of an absorption-level interaction.
flashcards.back: Tetracyclines + calcium/iron/antacids → chelation prevents absorption.

mcqs.q: Which of the following drugs increases the absorption of Propranolol and Paracetamol by increasing gastric motility?
mcqs.explanation: Metoclopramide accelerates gastric emptying, increasing absorption of Propranolol and Paracetamol. Atropine has the opposite effect.

mcqs.q: A patient taking Rifampin is also using oral contraceptives. What is the expected outcome?
options: Increased efficacy of the oral contraceptives
No effect on the oral contraceptives
Contraceptive failure due to CYP enzyme induction
mcqs.explanation: Rifampin is a potent inducer of CYP2C and CYP3A; it increases the metabolism of contraceptive hormones, reducing their efficacy and causing contraceptive failure.

mcqs.q: Why should Tetracycline not be given with aluminium-containing antacids?
options: Because antacids raise gastric pH, reducing Tetracycline absorption only
Because antacids chelate Tetracycline and prevent its absorption
There is no interaction between them
mcqs.explanation: Mg++, Al+++, and Ca++ ions chelate Tetracycline and Ciprofloxacin, preventing their absorption from the gastrointestinal tract.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an inhibitor of hepatic CYP450 enzymes?
mcqs.explanation: Ketoconazole is a CYP450 inhibitor. Phenytoin, Rifampin, and Carbamazepine are enzyme inducers.

mcqs.q: A patient taking an MAOI eats a large amount of cheese. What is the likely danger?
mcqs.explanation: Cheese contains tyramine, which is normally broken down by MAO. With MAO inhibited, tyramine accumulates and releases large amounts of catecholamines, causing an acute hypertensive crisis.

mcqs.q: What is the dangerous interaction between MAOI and SSRI?
mcqs.explanation: Combining MAOI and SSRI causes a dangerous accumulation of serotonin leading to serotonin syndrome, which can be life-threatening.

mcqs.q: What is the purpose of giving Probenecid with Penicillin?
options: To prolong the effect of Penicillin by inhibiting its renal secretion
To improve Penicillin distribution into tissues
mcqs.explanation: Probenecid inhibits the active tubular secretion of Penicillin in the kidneys, prolonging its half-life and raising its blood level.

mcqs.q: Which of the following increases urinary excretion of Amphetamine (a weak base)?
options: Alkalinising the urine with sodium bicarbonate
Acidifying the urine with ammonium chloride
mcqs.explanation: Acidifying the urine converts weak bases (such as amphetamine) to their ionised form, which cannot be reabsorbed, thereby increasing their renal excretion (ion trapping).

mcqs.q: What is the combined effect of Verapamil with beta-blockers?
options: Severe bradycardia that may progress to cardiac arrest
mcqs.explanation: Both slow the heart rate; combining them may cause severe bradycardia or asystole.

mcqs.q: A patient taking 6-mercaptopurine for leukaemia is started on Allopurinol for gout. What is the expected outcome?
options: Increased 6-MP accumulation and worsened bone marrow suppression
mcqs.explanation: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase, which metabolises 6-MP, causing its dangerous accumulation and worsening bone marrow suppression. The 6-MP dose must be reduced.

mcqs.q: Rifampicin reduces the efficacy of Warfarin because it is:
options: It displaces Warfarin from protein binding
mcqs.explanation: Rifampicin is a potent enzyme inducer that accelerates the metabolism of Warfarin.

mcqs.q: Cimetidine raises the concentration of other drugs because it is:
mcqs.explanation: Cimetidine is an enzyme inhibitor that slows the metabolism of other drugs.

mcqs.q: Tetracycline absorption is reduced when taken with milk because of:
mcqs.explanation: It binds calcium, forming a non-absorbable complex.

high_yield.point: محفّزات الإنزيمات (Inducers) تُسرّع استقلاب الأدوية وتُقلل فعاليتها → فشل حبوب منع الحمل.
مثبّطات الإنزيمات (Inhibitors) ترفع تركيز الدواء → سُمّية (مثل Cimetidine + Warfarin).
Tetracyclines + الكالسيوم/الحديد/مضادات الحموضة → chelation يمنع الامتصاص.
التداخل قد يكون pharmacokinetic (ADME) أو pharmacodynamic (نفس/معاكس التأثير).

comparison_tables.title: محفّزات مقابل مثبّطات إنزيمات الكبد (CYP450)
rows: Rifampicin, Phenytoin, Carbamazepine, Barbiturates, مزمن الكحول
Cimetidine, Ketoconazole, Erythromycin, عصير الجريب فروت
